title: Count Positive Numbers
description: Given an array of numbers, return the count of elements that are strictly greater than zero. Zero itself does not count as positive.
whatShouldHappen: Loop through every element in the array.
If the current element is greater than zero, increment the counter.
Return the counter after the loop ends.
patternExplanation: Maintain a running tally and increment it only when a condition is met — the foundation of every counting loop.

title: Product of Array
description: Given an array of numbers, return the product of all elements multiplied together. Return 1 for an empty array.
whatShouldHappen: Start with a running product of 1 (the multiplicative identity).
Multiply the running product by the current element.
Return the product after the loop ends.
patternExplanation: Carry a running value through the loop and update it each iteration — the same pattern as summing, but with multiplication.

title: First Even Number
description: Given an array of integers, return the first even number found from left to right. Return -1 if the array contains no even numbers.
whatShouldHappen: Loop through the array from left to right.
Check if the current element is divisible by 2 (i.e. even).
If it is, return it immediately — no need to continue.
If the loop finishes without finding one, return -1.
patternExplanation: Return the moment a condition is satisfied so the loop does no extra work — the pattern behind Array.prototype.find.

title: Sum of Squares
description: Given an array of numbers, return the sum of the square of every element. Squaring a negative number always produces a positive result.
whatShouldHappen: Start a running total at zero.
Square the current element and add it to the total.
Return the total after the loop ends.
patternExplanation: Apply a transformation to each element and accumulate the results — the same pattern powers reduce with a custom operation.

title: All Positive
description: Given an array of numbers, return true if every element is strictly greater than zero. Return false as soon as a non-positive element is found. An empty array returns true.
whatShouldHappen: If any element is zero or negative, return false immediately.
If the loop completes without finding a non-positive element, return true.
patternExplanation: Return false on the first counterexample and true only if the whole array passes — this is the pattern behind Array.prototype.every.
